codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully introduces first-class worktree persistence and session linkage with durable worktree IDs. The worktree domain model is well-designed with clear lifecycle states (creating/active/archived/cleanup_pending/cleanup_failed/removed). The persistence layer uses JSONL with proper Zod validation, and the session-worktree linkage via worktreeId enables reliable rehydration by ID rather than path inference.

issues.description: The detectProjectRoot() method duplicates git repository detection logic that exists in RepoContext. Consider injecting RepoContext or sharing the detection utility to avoid drift between implementations.

Legacy sessions without worktreeId are silently skipped during rehydration. While this is documented behavior, consider logging or surfacing metrics about how many legacy sessions were filtered to aid in debugging during migration periods.

createOrphanedSessionRecord() marks orphaned sessions as failed in persistence but does not emit any events or logs. Consider adding visibility when orphaned sessions are detected and marked as recovered.

The isRuduManaged field defaults to true but is not marked as readonly in the interface even though it represents a creation-time invariant. Consider making this readonly in the domain model to prevent accidental mutation.

Test 'creates session with worktreeId when provided in metadata' notes that queueSession doesn't extract worktreeId from metadata. This is an incomplete test - it should either test the actual worktree linkage mechanism or be removed if the feature is implemented elsewhere.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worktree persistence follows the same JSONL pattern as session persistence, but there's no documented convention for when to use JSONL vs other storage formats. The pattern is now established across two domains (sessions and worktrees) and should be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Both SyncJsonlSessionRepository and SyncJsonlWorktreeRepository use identical append-only JSONL with last-write-wins folding. See src/services/persistence/SyncJsonlWorktreeRepository.ts:86-102 for the folding logic that mirrors session persistence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worktree lifecycle states (creating/active/archived/cleanup_pending/cleanup_failed/removed) are domain invariants that should be documented in .factory/library/worktree-domain.md. The current documentation mentions lifecycle semantics but doesn't enumerate the actual state machine.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/domain/worktree.ts:10-17 defines the lifecycle states but .factory/library/worktree-domain.md only describes archive/delete semantics without referencing the actual state values that will be persisted.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There's an established pattern for repository interfaces (SessionRepository, WorktreeRepository) with corresponding Noop implementations for testing, but no base class or shared utilities. The boilerplate for CRUD operations and in-memory test implementations is repeated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Compare src/services/persistence/SessionRepository.ts:37-54 and src/services/persistence/WorktreeRepository.ts:38-52 - both have nearly identical Noop implementations. A shared Repository<T> base interface could reduce duplication.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project uses Bun as the runtime (evident from bun.lock and package.json) but .factory/services.yaml only documents commands, not the runtime requirement. Workers discovering the project should know Bun is required.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml lists 'bun test' and 'bun dev' commands but doesn't declare Bun as a service dependency or document the minimum version requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly handles the relationship between WorktreeRepository and SessionRepository during rehydration, but this dependency ordering (worktrees must be loaded before sessions can be validated) isn't documented in AGENTS.md or the library.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/SessionManager.ts:1058-1074 shows that knownWorktreeIds must be collected before filtering sessions, but this sequencing constraint isn't mentioned in architectural documentation.

summary: This feature implementation is solid and meets all requirements from VAL-FOUND-004. The worktree persistence model is well-designed with proper lifecycle states, the session-worktree linkage via durable worktreeId works correctly, and rehydration properly filters legacy and orphaned sessions. The 23 new tests provide good coverage of CRUD operations, persistence, and graph reconstruction. Minor observations about code sharing and documentation gaps were noted but none are blocking. The implementation enables future multi-session-per-worktree support as intended.
